entry.title: Re: What 20+ Years of Working with SAP Has Taught Me About Successful Transformations
entry.content: Very well articulated, the post captures the importance of business change adoption over technology. The brilliant design may fail because its complex for users!

entry.title: Re: Design Question – Compensation vs Variable Pay
entry.content: HI Xavier, Thank you so much for your answer. From a Variable Pay configuration perspective, is it possible to ignore salary history when an employee remains in the same Employment Type throughout the performance year, while still recognizing salary and AIP target percentage changes when the employee moves from a Non-Executive to an Executive Employment Type? In other words, can Variable Pay support using the final salary for standard cases, but use historical salary and target percentage information only when a Non-Executive to Executive Employment Type change occurs?

entry.title: Custom scale base and custom calculation base issue in SAP TM
entry.content:
Hello 

We are defining a custom calculation base and custom scale base. As per SAP TM  Enhancement guide below web dynpro component is missing in our system. Can you pls advice why this is so and what is the new component ? we are on S/4 HANA embedded system 2023

2nd question -do u have a KBA which provides information on how to define a custom scale base and custom calculation base.

Thanks for helping out so far, now endpoint details are showing in manage service registry. but while I am trying to run the URL using the postman than its not identifying the path.

can you please help to get the https://<host>:50061/ what should be hostname to run it using postman.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: How to build a Custom Hierarchy in ABAP CDS views
entry.content:
@former_member205880  Did you manage to find out a solution for this issue ?

entry.title: SAP Time: Employee generated Absence Quota while on LOA
entry.content:
HI,

An employee on LOA  generated Absence Quota of 0.4 PTO hours, he should have been 0. All other employees on LOA works fine but this one. Has anyone experienced this scenario before? I am trying to figure out what triggered it to generate 0.4 hour instead of 0.

Thanks in anticipation. 

SAP on Premise.

entry.title: How did the field - Note in VKM1 gets filled in VKM1
entry.content: Need to understand the source of the field - Note in VKM1. And for few cases, that field is not filled. Business would like to have that field filled for all entries we get in VKM1

entry.title: Re: managesalespricelistin2 - ReleaseStatusCode on Produc...
entry.content:
Hello Mark,

 

Currently, we do not support CRUD (Create, Read, Update, and Delete) operations for OData on Sales Price Specification. The PriceList BO (Business Object) is not write enabled due to complexity issues and technical limitations. As a result, the functionality to write to the BO is not enabled at this time. However, the customer can still query the BO using GET requests. OData is a stateless request, and because of this, we do not initialize the BO, which is necessary for these operations.

Please see also KBA 3191877 - The Item Cannot be Deleted from the Price List using ODATA

 

With kind regards,

Kathrin

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Connection To Sharepoint via Open Connectors
entry.content: Hi Andrew, I thank you for your advice. These steps of setting is functional. I was able to establish connection successfully.

entry.title: Re: Reopen a completed sales order
entry.content:
Hello Andras,

 

It seems that the Delivery Rule Single Delivery was used for the Item in the Sales Order.

In this case only one delivery may be sent to the customer, this means that only one confirmation is created. The requested delivery date does not have to be met, and the delivery may comprise the requested quantity or only part of the requested quantity.

In this case the only option is to create a new Sales Order for the Product with the remaining quantity that needs to be delivered.

 

The following KBA explains the difference for the Single Delivery options available in the system:

KBA 2689993 - Single Delivery Options for a Sales Order Item

 

With kind regards,

Kathrin

entry.title: Re: how to custom agent for sales price approval
entry.content:
Good day @fptappuser

Yes, your understanding is partly correct.

If hardcoding value 101 in RSM_BADI_CONTEXT_ATTRIB_CALC caused only matching team members to receive the approval, then this proves that the custom responsibility definition can influence team-based agent determination in your sales price workflow scenario.

So, for scenario 1, the likely issue is not the ream setup itself. The issue is probably that the real runtime value for Price Channel is either not filled, filled with a value that does not match the team responsibility value, not available in the responsibility context at runtime, or not returned correctly by the BAdI.

Basically, your test with hardcoded 101 is a good proof point. Now you need to debug or trace what value is actually calculated for Price Channel during workflow runtime.

For Scenario 2, RSM_BADI_RESPONSIBILITY_RULE is the correct area to check if you want a fully custom agent determination rule. Consult the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/2ddc2320faf840929362c58ece6b8c12.html?locale=en-US  

However, the key limitation is this:

  • You can only use CreatedByUser if the sales price workflow context provides it, or if you can derive it through a released API or released busines object available to the BAdI.
  • If CreatedByUser is not available in the context and there is no released way to read it, then creator-based routing is not cleanly supported.
  • If it is available, then you your mapping logic “Creator A → Approver B” can be implemented through the custom responsibility rule.

entry.title: Re: Price of goods mvt will become negative in goods rece...
entry.content:
Below are the key causes for your issue

1) Valuation inconsistency
The system calculates the value of the goods movement based on PO price, GR quantity, and existing stock valuation. If this calculation results in a negative value, SAP blocks the posting.

2) Moving Average Price (MAP) issue
If the material is managed with MAP, and the GR quantity/value combination would drive the MAP negative, the error occurs.

3) Unit of Measure mismatch
Even if quantities and values look identical, a difference in base UoM vs order UoM can cause valuation errors.

4) Previous postings
Check if earlier GRs or IRs for this PO line item had partial postings that changed the valuation unexpectedly.

5) Currency or exchange rate differences
If the PO currency differs from company code currency, rounding or exchange rate differences can trigger the error.

In your case, since quantity and value are identical (14,524.720), the likely culprit is either MAP being zero/very low or a UoM conversion mismatch. I would recommend first checking the material’s valuation in MMBE/MM03 and simulating the GR in MIGO to see the exact calculated value.

entry.title: Re: How did the field - Note in VKM1 gets filled in VKM1
entry.content: Which field you are referring?

entry.title: TDT and RITA Configuration Check results in inconsistency
entry.content:
Hi,

We are doing a Test data Refresh to refresh data from S/4 Production to S/4 Quality. The specific nuance in our case is that the production system is blank and we have not sent any transports (including CBC). When we execute the readiness check report we get an error - 

TDT and RITA Configuration Check
The source and target systems must match in terms of their TDT and RITA configurations.

The problem is to resolve this we will need to send the full set of transports from QA to Prod which we cannot do. Has some one seen this before?

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required for ASN Field CDS View
entry.content:
Hello @sakar_korde94c0f8 

Thank you for your question.

The duplicate check requirement cannot be solved only by finding a CDS view.

The ASN/External ID in the EWM inbound delivery is sourced from the LE inbound delivery. The ASN filed in Change Inbound Deliveries, is the reference number for the External ID from the logistics Execution delivery, and it i not editable in the cloud warehouse management app.

In simple terms:

  • For inbound delivery, first check released CDS view I_InboundDelivery.
  • For item level data, check I_InboundDeliveyItem.                                                         
  • For EWM, check the released VDM views Warehouse Inbound Delivery Header and Warehouse Inbound Delivery Item in View Browser.
  • The field you are looking for is the delivery External ID/ASN references, technically linked to the LE external delivery reference.
  • See KBA 3057820 on details about LIFEX and its usage.

However, for preventing duplicates:

  • CDS views are read only and cannot by themselves stop the user from saving a duplicate value.
  • If you manually enter the External ID in a delivery app, you need a released validation BAdI for the inbound delivery save process. If no released BAdI exists for this field and process, then this duplicate blocking is not supported directly in standard cloud extensibility.
  • If you create the ASN through an interface, the better design is to validate the duplicate externally before creating or updating the inbound delivery.

See the following reference for more insights.

3669017 - ASN field in "Change Inbound Deliveries – Deliveries" App is non-editable in SAP S/4hana cloud public edition - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Quantity conversion management between PC/KG in sales order/delivery
entry.content:
In the sales process, the order is entered in pieces (PC/PZ), while logistics execution and warehouse goods issue must reflect the actual weight in KG communicated by the warehouse.
At item level in the sales document, there is a quantity conversion field between the commercial unit of measure and the weight unit of measure (example shown: 10 PC ↔ 5 KG).
For reference, we are using:

Sales Order: 202
Material: 97

However, the system does not allow the correct handling of decimal values / decimal separators in the conversion ratio, preventing the entry of actual quantities such as, for example:

10 PC = 5.4 KG
1 PC = 0.540 KG

As a consequence, the system continues to use a theoretical or rigidly determined conversion, with an impact on the actual logistics data.

We would like to verify whether there is a standard way in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to:

  • manually update the PC/KG conversion field with the actual value received from the warehouse;
  • manage decimal values in the conversion ratio;

entry.title: Re: Design Question – Compensation vs Variable Pay
entry.content: @mfpabon23 if you customize everything which is very difficult, yes. In standard no, you would need 2 templates.

entry.title: Re: BW Modernization - Shift Infoproviders from BW PCE to...
entry.content: After the BW PCE shutdown the local tables (files) become effectively a snapshot data product. They remain queryable, shareable via Delta Share, and consumable by SAC/Databricks/Snowflake, but no longer refreshing unless re-sourced.

entry.title: Re: Querying SAP SuccessFactors in Natural Language with Claude Desktop and the SF-MCP Server
entry.content:
@HarshPatelV 

Glad you brought this up! Reducing cognitive load is really the whole point here. And yeah, abstraction only works when it actually matches how people think otherwise it just moves the problem around.

What challenges have you run into doing this in practice?

entry.title: Re: Best compatible Warehouse scanners and label printers...
entry.content:
Hello!

SAP does not publish a certified list of approved scanner or printer models for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Compatibility is determined by protocol and browser requirements, not by device brand or model.

SAP GUI for HTML is used for mobile devices in Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The personalization of RF interfaces can be done with SAP Screen Personas. There is no ITSmobile, no RFC connection, and no communication arrangement needed, the RF framework runs on HTTPS via a dedicated URL on cloud tenant. The physical scanner hardware is secondary, what matters is whether the device can run a supported industrial browser that correctly renders SAP GUI for HTML.

Ivanti Velocity 2.1 has achieved SAP certification as integrated with SAP S/4HANA, supporting SAP Extended Warehouse Management and the browser apps for the mobile internet transaction server (ITS) component. Ivanti Velocity web browser is available for Android, iOS, and Windows 10 devices.

The most commonly referenced validated device in SAP KBAs and community guidance is the Zebra MC9300 running Android 10 with Ivanti Velocity 2.1.17

Other devices used in practice (not SAP-certified, but community-validated with Ivanti Velocity):

Zebra TC52, TC57, TC72, TC77 - Android-based rugged handhelds, widely deployed

Honeywell CT60, CK65 - compatible when running Ivanti Velocity on Android

Datalogic Memor 20 - also used in SAP EWM environments

Before purchasing hardware, always validate your chosen device using the Test RF Environment app available in quality system. It is the responsibility of the implementation team to verify device compatibility and connection stability before go-live.

 

An example will be the following:

RF Scanners (mobile data terminals):

RF Framework: SAP GUI for HTML over HTTPS. No special configuration needed, runs entirely via browser

Certified browser: Ivanti Velocity 2.1+. This is the ONLY SAP-certified industrial browser for Public Edition RF

Scanner OS: Android 10 or later

Most referenced scanner: Zebra MC9300 running Android 10 with Ivanti Velocity 2.1.17; this is the combination explicitly referenced in SAP KBAs

Other community validated scanners: Zebra TC52, TC57, TC72, TC77;  Honeywell CT60, CK65;  Datalogic Memor 20. All require Ivanti Velocity installed on the device.

Barcode standard: GS1-128 natively supported. Other types are configurable within the RF framework.

Label printers:

Printing architecture: SAP Cloud Print Manager (CPM) in pull integration mode,  this is mandatory in Public Edition.

Recommended print format: PDF, broadest compatibility, primary recommended approach for all printer brands.

ZPL / Native Zebra Print: limited to three formats only: Zebra 203 dpi, 300 dpi, and 600 dpi. PDF is preferred.

Fixed label printers: Zebra ZD230, ZD420, ZD621 for desktop use.  Zebra ZT230, ZT410, ZT610 for industrial/high-volume use.

Mobile label printers: Zebra ZQ521 and ZQ621, connect via Bluetooth to the scanner device for on-the-floor label printing.

 

SAP Reference Documents

KBA 3671646 — FAQ: Usage of RF Devices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (primary reference, actively maintained by SAP)

KBA 3048632 — Information and Restrictions for Mobile Data Terminals in Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud (covers device and browser requirements in full)

KBA 3672406 — Resource Management for RF Devices in Fiori Apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/supply-chain-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-2108/ba-p/13519983#2.1.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/e9c79a81dffa48c2b9d76f41875e1cb7.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/67b4cbca851448969eaf130327ad959f.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2602.500&q=Activate%20Radio%20Frequency%20Log

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/mobile-warehousing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-warehouse-management/ba-p/13533791

https://www.ivanti.com/use-cases/sap-certified-supply-chain-solutions

https://www.ivanti.com/resources/v/doc/ivi/2450/c7930814b791

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Re: Querying SAP SuccessFactors in Natural Language with Claude Desktop and the SF-MCP Server
entry.content:
@Wendi 

Yes, totally valid concern! Basic Auth will stop working once SAP deprecates it (Nov 15th 2026), so this needs to be addressed. The good news, OAuth 2.0 support is already in the works, it's not a big rebuild, more of a few update. Follow the GitHub repo (github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp) and you'll get notified once it's live.

entry.title: upgrade of SAP portal netweaver and LDAP issues
entry.content:
Hi,

We have upgraded our SAP portal netweaver from SP22 to SP34 plugged to a SAP BW system upgraded also from SP5 to SP34 and we have now LDAP issues(timeout, connection closed/aborted, query rejected) only when a lot of users are connected.

We have the error message "502 bad gateway" in the SAP portal . 

No problem noticed with the non sap other applications that use the same LDAP.

Do you know if something has changed, security or other, with these 12 support packages ?

Regards.

Catherine.

entry.title: product Revision level
entry.content:
Hi All, Please Suggest.

I’m working on some post migration tasks one of them is mass load of Product Revision Level.

In ECC we used External Revision Levels allowing us to enter any level we wanted.

P1 – P5 was for preproduction product then it would get a number or another alphanumeric code.

In S4 public cloud is there a configuration that will allow us to use any revision level we want…

If I removed all the Revision Levels will that allow us the use anything we want???

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Identifying integration systems details using SFAPI A...
entry.content:
SFAPI is not fully deprecated. Compound Employee API, which is part of SFAPI, is still supported and heavily used. It is also used in standard integrations to SAP payroll, e.g. EC Payroll (ECP) or SAP HCM/S4 payroll (integration packages PA_SE_IN and H4S4HCM).  Compound Employee calls  appear  in the SFAPI log with  SFAPI Call = Query and Object = compoundemployee. 

User name PTP_SFAPI might indicate, that this is the point-to-point connection to ECP, for which the use of SFAPI/Compound Employee API is standard, i.e. there would be no need to change anything. 

In general I would recommend to connect with your integration team, in order to find out, which systems are connected to SFSF.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
entry.content: 8e0a18ea561212cf650669b2f3b160f2a85a9f1ac4cad09784eafd5fe675c3b6

entry.title: Re: How did the field - Note in VKM1 gets filled in VKM1
entry.content:
To which field in VBKRED are you referring ?

For empty fields, refer to  111425 - Fields of VBKRED not filled CMKUA  for suggested solutions, including:

  • Using the user exit VBKRED_EX1 and the FORM routine USER_EXIT_FUELLEN_XVBKRED in program DBKMVF02 to declare and populate user-defined fields (Note 110311). Ensure that VBKRED contains document header data.

entry.title: Re: 外向交货单80006071无法过账发货;显示被锁定交货，VLA019
entry.content:
Hello!

The message type OB_DLV_CHA/00001  is the outbound delivery change message, meaning S/4HANA sent a change notification for delivery 80006071 to EWM, and EWM has locked the delivery to process that update. If that update did not complete cleanly, the lock remains.

Resolution steps:

  1. Before taking any action, wait 5–10 minutes and try posting the goods issue again from the Delivery Monitor. In many cases the lock releases automatically once the background processing of the OB_DLV_CHA message completes. If it clears, no further action is needed.
  2. Check the message dashboard for a stuck message: if the lock persists, go back to the Message Dashboard app and check the status of message OB_DLV_CHA/00001 for delivery 80006071. Check if the message is in error status. If so, there is an unprocessed queue entry that is holding the lock. The entry must be resolved before the lock can be released. Or if the message in Processing status with no progress indicates the background job is blocked.
  3.  Check the message queue in the warehouse monitor. An unprocessed entry for delivery may prevent the persistent lock from being released.
  4.  Check for an open warehouse task or active process in EWM. The lock can also be held if is an active warehouse task or warehouse order still open for the delivery in EWM that has not been confirmed.

 

More details here:

KBA 3263839 — Not able to release unpacked stock / GI issues in EWM S/4HANA Cloud (component SCM-EWM-DLP-2CL)

2749123 - PDO/PDI XXX is locked with a persistent lock by an other process

entry.title: Re: FINAL PAYGUIDE FIELDS NOT UPDATING WHEN A PROMOTION T...
entry.content: This is not a bug.  The system will not update until the new "promoted" field values are written to the database.

entry.title: Clarification on Best Practices for Activating Additional Ledger (2L) in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP community

We are currently reviewing our fixed assets setup in SAP. Since Go live, only the leading Ledger 0L has been active and used for asset accounting postings.

We are now conseidering using ledger 2L and depreciation area 15 for tax reporting purposes and we would appreciate your guidance on the recommended approach. 

Specifically, we would like to confirm:

1. Best Practice Approach

  • What is SAP’s recommended best practice to work with a new depreciation area linked to the ledger 2L after go-live in fexed Asset

2. Data Replication Capability

  • Does SAP provide any standard functionality in S/4HANA Public Cloud to automatically replicate or copy historical data (e.g., depreciation) from depreciation area 1 to tax depreciation area

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
entry.content: cb789bbda6fc60512c3d3dd36caabeb7d373fdc9006aa010bc79ad682b49c5fb

entry.title: Re: Recurring Payments from SAP to EC
entry.content:
Infotype 0014 corresponds to Recurring Pay and Recurring Deductions portlets in EC.

Data can be send from SAP HCM to EC via different ways: 

  • Standard interface package (infoporter) , see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL_INTEGRATION_TO_SAP_BUSINESS_SUITE/602c38f7400b4d2693602a178c2bf028?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2605
  • Custom build interface 
  • CSV import

It depends on the concrete use case and requirements, which of the above fits best. 

Interfaces into Recurring Pay should take into account the following in order to avoid unwanted data losses or inconsistencies: 

  • Basic pay (infotype 0008) and recurring pay (infotype 0014) are stored in the same Recurring Pay portlet in EC
  • Recurring pay can potentially be updated by different processes,e.g. manual updates in EC during hiring/promotion, annual compensation review from Compensation Management, automated pay-scale increase etc.  --> find out which process are relevant in your context and make sure that interfaces are designed accordingly

entry.title: Re: Asset Impairment / Asset Revaluation Configuration - SAP S4Hana
entry.content:
Hello Gowresh,

Thanks for your valuable explanation on the Asset impairment. I need a small clarification from your side. As even I have implemented the same steps which you have provided above. We are using S/4 HANA 2021 Onpremises edition.

Here, even though I have assigned

 - Z81 - Impairment loss current year

- Trans type group - 82

- Cons trans type - Z28 (this is the desired outcome which my client is expecting).

However, when i complete the depreciation run it is posting the line with standard trans type 120 instead of Z28. But in your above example i notice you have provided Trans type 943 and the final doc posted lines are also 943. 

Can you please help me in this last step where I'm unable to get the Z28. please advise.

Thanks,

Hemanth

entry.title: Re: Offline document upload and download in SSAM 2310
entry.content: Sorry, you will probably need to open a support case. I am not familiar with the Open Text integration aspects.

entry.title: Re: Portugal – Support for Original / Duplicate / Triplic...
entry.content:
Hello!

SAP provide a partial standard mechanism and supported extensibility paths that together can address the requirement.

SAP has a built-in Original/Duplicate watermark mechanism for billing document output, documented in KBA 3023958. This works as follows:

When a billing document is printed for the first time, the system passes a flag (IV_DUPLICATE_INDICATOR) into the Adobe Form at render time. On the first print this flag is blank, the form displays no watermark or displays "Original." On any subsequent reprint, the flag is set and the form displays "Duplicate."

This mechanism is already implemented in the standard form rendering pipeline. This is country-activated,  SAP only enables it for countries where it is legally required. Portugal is not currently in the list of activated countries. The standard Original/Duplicate distinction can be activated for Portugal by raising a SAP support message requesting that Portugal be added to the country activation table. This is a configuration-only change on SAP's side. Reference KBA 3023958 when opening the message.

To extend to triplicate and full reprint tracking, the standard framework is binary: it only distinguishes original from duplicate. To cover the full requirement including triplicate labeling and sequential reprint tracking, two additional components are needed, both within the supported Public Edition extensibility model:

Step 1.  Add a print counter field using BAdI SD_BIL_PRINT_STANDARD_HEAD

Using the Custom Fields and Logic app, create a custom field on the billing document (Business Context: Sales: Billing Document) to store a print counter. Implement the BAdI SD_BIL_PRINT_STANDARD_HEAD to increment this counter each time an output request is created for the document: 1 for the first print, 2 for the first reprint, 3 for the second reprint, and so on. This counter is then exposed to the Adobe Form through the OData data source. This approach is documented in KBA 2679030.

Step 2.  Drive the copy label in the Adobe Form using LiveCycle Designer scripting (the setting are documented in KBA 2984817). Open the billing document form template in Adobe LiveCycle Designer . Bind a text field to the counter value from the data source. Add a JavaScript or FormCalc script at form level,  for example:

if CopyNumber == 1 then "Original"

else if CopyNumber == 2 then "Duplicate"

else if CopyNumber >= 3 then "Triplicate"

For same run copies (all three copies printed simultaneously on first print). If your business process requires all three copies to be produced in a single print run at the time the invoice is first issued, rather than through sequential reprints,  this can be achieved within Adobe LiveCycle Designer by creating three separate master pages, each with its own label (Original, Duplicate, Triplicate), and assigning each master page to the corresponding copy number via the form's pagination settings. The constraint is that this only works when all copies are generated in one output run.

 

KBA 3023958  Watermark Duplicate/Original for Billing Document Output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (standard mechanism, country activation, Customer Influence request)

KBA 3596806  Printing Multiple Copies of Billing Document in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Original, Duplicate, Triplicate  confirmed gap and paths)

KBA 2679030   Extensibility for Billing Document Printout in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (BAdI SD_BIL_PRINT_STANDARD_HEAD and Custom Fields)

KBA 2984817  Form Design in Adobe LiveCycle Designer (scripting, form templates, merge  the technical reference for form-level implementation)

entry.title: How to avoid 5-digit UoM limit using chained alternative units in S/4?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m currently trying to better understand how Alternative Units of Measure (AUoM) work in SAP S/4HANA, especially in more complex scenarios.

From my understanding:

  • Each material has a Base Unit of Measure (BUoM)
  • Alternative units are defined with a conversion factor to the base unit
  • All quantities are internally converted to the base unit for stock management.

 In my case, I need to model large conversions, but I want to avoid exceeding SAP limits in numerator/denominator (max ~5 digits).

For example:

  • 1 PALLET = 1,000,000 PCS → ❌ not allowed directly
  • So I’m considering breaking into intermediate units:
    • 1 BOX = 1,000 PCS
    • 1 PALLET = 1,000 BOX

 

When defining unit of measure conversions, what is the correct process in SAP S/4HANA?
 
Thanks in advance.
 
Regards.

entry.title: Bonus Assignment Statements-Custom Fields
entry.content:
Hi Experts

I am facing a issue with outputting  custom fields created in Variable Pay module on the Bonus Assignment statement ( it is a custom XSLT statement).  These custom fields have been created at the "Business Goal" level using lookup table method as the source of information.

The following SAP defined steps have been followed:

  1. Import Var Pay relevant employee and other data from EC( Employee History)
  2. Set up Var Pay system in the Var Pay module
  3. Run Bonus calculation
  4. Generate Bonus Assignment statement

What is a puzzling behaviour is that when above mentioned steps are followed the "Bonus Assignment statement" does get generated but without any of the Custom fields.

In addition to the above mentioned, if I launch the worksheet (not desirable) and then generate the "Bonus Assignment statement" then these Custom fields do get reflected on the statement.

As there is bit of nebulousness in the standard SAP documentation on Bonus assignment statements, I am seeking your help to get to the desired state of getting these custom fields displayed on the "Bonus Assignment" statement without the unnecessary launch of any worksheets/forms.

Thanks

entry.title: Connection failure for TGKTST on DBW system (Host: vhpdphbddb01)
entry.content:
A connection issue is being reported from TGKTST on the DBW system on vhpdphbddb01.

A database error occured. The error message in the database was: "internal error: Remote Source [TGKTST] is not accessible: [...xODBC][Driver Manager]Can't open lib '/opt/microsoft/msodbcsql18/lib64/libmsodbcsql-18.4.so.1.1' : file not found" 

Could you please verify if the ODBC driver is correctly installed on the host?

entry.title: Re: FBl1 and FBL1n 1000 of layout are missing
entry.content: Is dynpro 1000 missing in some selection variant, or did user lost some display variant named 1000 ?

entry.title: Re: GBP vs USD currency total not matching with exchange...
entry.content: Try to sum separately debit and credit, with rounding such result is possible.

entry.title: Mass upload Data issue
entry.content: Hi, Our data loading Team is looking for FU_CODE field for Return order MSR_S_SALES_ITEM - Data element MSR_FU_CODE , dynpro field GS_MSR_SALES_ITEM-FU_CODE, in which table this values are stored, where can we find relation between return sales order and FU_CODE.

entry.title: We want to know where FU_CODE Follow up action code and return order is stored in table
entry.content: Hi, Our data loading Team is looking for FU_CODE field for Return order MSR_S_SALES_ITEM - Data element MSR_FU_CODE , dynpro field GS_MSR_SALES_ITEM-FU_CODE, in which table this values are stored, where can we find relation between return sales order and FU_CODE.

entry.title: Re: Trial Cloud Identity Services: first administrator never received activation email
entry.content: I am also facing same issue.. please let me know if you know solution.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Next Era of ABAP Development
entry.content:
Hey,

this would be a great option for SAP customers to leverage their ABAP development. But there is a huge thing I can't understand at all: The capabilities are provided in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition beginning from release 2025. We are still using an onPrem system, which is on the same release level (2025 FPS00) and we have all the options to create the destionations and certificates to ABAP AI capabilities. So technically there is everything established, no restrictions are made, but we still can't use the AI capabilities at all with those onPrem systems. So, as cool as this stuff is on BAS and so on, our core can't benefit from it so we will not buy/subscribe to this. Thats pretty sad for us as a customer and sad for SAP as well.

entry.title: Re: New MSO version 2604 build 199929.20172
entry.content: Now Microsoft also excepted outage. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/office/dev/add-ins/resources/resources-office-add-in-known-issues

entry.title: Re: Bonus Assignment Statements-Custom Fields
entry.content:
Hi @rkumar1947 

Answer from our SAP reporting expert who builds these custom statements or bonus assignment letters for North America projects: 

"short answer, its a mapping issue.   for most var pay statements, you have to run a script to pull data from the forms to reveal the proper mapping. It shows fine when you launch forms because the forms aren't custom designed.  the script you run actually pulls the form "skeleton" so you find where everything really sits.

 We do not provide this script unfortunately.

 Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2025 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content: Appliance ID is 225615502

entry.title: Live Versions for Planning Models in SAP Analytics Cloud: Microsoft Azure Is Here!
entry.content:
Introduction

The live data access story for SAP Analytics Cloud planning models keeps getting stronger. When we introduced live versions in QRC4 2025, we opened the door for planning models to connect directly to live SQL sources without replication. Google BigQuery was the first supported source, followed by Snowflake in QRC1 2026. With this release, Microsoft Azure joins the portfolio — and the pattern should by now be a familiar one.

If you missed the earlier posts in this series, here is a quick recap: live versions allow a planning model in SAP Analytics Cloud to reference fact data directly from an external source at query time, without importing or replicating that data into SAC. The live data is read-only — you cannot write back to the source — but you can use it in calculations, data actions, and stories, and copy it into planning versions when needed. This applies to SAC models that are not deployed to SAP Datasphere. For seamless planning scenarios, live connectivity to SAP Datasphere is the way to go. Learn more:

  • Live Versions from an SQL Source for SAP Analytics Cloud Planning
  • SAP Analytics Cloud Planning with Live Snowflake Data
  • Unlocking the Next Chapter of Seamless Planning in SAP Business Data Cloud with Live Versions

With Microsoft Azure now supported, customers running their data on Azure can bring that data directly into their SAC planning models without any replication pipeline. Let's walk through how it works.

 

How Does It Work?

There are two ways to get started with live versions from Microsoft Azure: you can create a new model directly from your Azure data, or add a live version to an existing model. We will cover both approaches.

Creating a New Model from Azure Data

Alternatively, you can create a planning model directly from your Azure data source — a clean approach when starting from scratch. The video example is based on Snowflake. It is the same workflow, just choose Azure instead.

  • Go to the Modeler and select Create Model
  • Choose Create Model from Data
  • Select Microsoft Azure — you can filter the connection list by Data Storage "Cloud" to find it quickly
  • Choose your connection and select a view
  • The model is created along the structure of the source view, with a live connection established immediately — visible in the data preview

From here, you work with the model as you would with any other SAC planning model. You can remove dimensions and measures you do not need, define calculations directly on the live data, and use data actions to copy actuals from Azure into a plan version stored in SAC's own fact table.

Working with the model

 

Use live version data in planning

Adding a Live Version to an Existing Model

Open your model and navigate to the Data section in the model details. You will find a new entry called External Live Version Data Sources.

From there, the steps are straightforward. The video example is based on Google BigQuery. It is the same workflow, just choose Azure instead.

  • Select Connect External Data Source
  • Choose an existing target version or create a blank one
  • Select Microsoft Azure as the source system
  • Choose your connection
  • Select your data source from Azure
  • Map the data source columns to your model columns — not every source or target column needs to be mapped, measures can be NULL, and dimensions can receive a default member
  • Review any performance hints or master data mismatch notifications — missing dimension members can be added directly from this dialog
  • Start using the live data in calculations, data actions, and stories

Add a live version to an existing model

You can add multiple live sources to a single model if needed.

 

FAQ

Where can I use this feature?

This feature is available for SAP Analytics Cloud models in SAP Analytics Cloud tenants running on HANA Cloud that are not deployed to SAP Datasphere (i.e. not using seamless planning).

Can I use any source view from Azure?

Yes, with the caveat that not all data types are supported. Please refer to the product documentation for the full list of supported data types.

What can I do with live versions — and what are the limitations?

You can:

  • Display live version data in the model data foundation and in tables, charts, and other widgets in stories
  • Reference live version data in model and story calculations as well as data actions, including advanced formulas

You cannot:

  • Write back to the Azure source directly. To persist planned data, copy the live version data into a planning version via copy/paste in the table, data actions, or version management — the plan data is then stored in the SAC fact table

How do I know if data is live or replicated?

In the model data preview, live versions are highlighted and the data source is shown per version in the version dimension. In stories, live version data is locked for data entry and formatted distinctly. Unplannable data reasons provide accurate context.

Conclusion

With Microsoft Azure joining Google BigQuery and Snowflake as a supported live source, SAP Analytics Cloud continues to expand its openness to the data platforms customers already use. Whether you are running your fact data in Azure, Snowflake, or BigQuery, you can now bring it directly into your planning model without any replication overhead — and without giving up the full power of SAC planning, calculations, and stories.

This is part of SAP Analytics Cloud's broader alignment with SAP Business Data Cloud's ambition to be a truly open platform. Follow the Roadmap Explorer to stay up to date on further additions to the live connectivity portfolio. Live connectivity to Microsoft SharePoint Excel files and Microsoft SharePoint Lists is currently planned for QRC4.2026!

Have questions or feedback? Drop them in the comments below.

entry.title: Re: How to find authorization roles with an object and specific field values tied together?
entry.content: To get the assigned users together with your authorization field values have a look at joining tables AGR_USERS and AGR_1251 in the quick viewer (SQVI) Once you've got your result set, sorting on the field AGR_1251-AUTH "Authorization name in user master maintenance" will help you to get the required field combinations.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
entry.content: a3d232e5bd2a39cb9a6130ca171f180cdf5455757e2c4ffd6bc833acbedff35f

entry.title: Re: ECP and S/4 HANA Payroll _ Saudi BADI
entry.content:
Unfortunately, SAP does not provide a centralized repository for all available BAdIs applicable to Saudi Arabia localization. To identify relevant BAdIs, use the following approach:

Transaction SE18 Search

Search for BAdI definitions using these patterns:

  • Badi name: *SA* and search for "KSA" or "SAUDI", and *PAYSA*
  • Enhancement Spot: *SA_* or *PAYSA*

Alternative Resources

  • SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com (search for "PY-SA")
  • SAP Support Portal: https://me.sap.com/home (search within PY-SA component)

Finally, about DMEE, SAP does not provide a Saudi Arabia-specific WPS BAdI equivalent to PADOM_WPS_FIELDS or PADQA_WPS_FIELDS as you already know.

For KSA localization requirements, you could consider implementing a custom DMEE tree .

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: Re: How I Connected Claude AI to My SAP ABAP System Using MCP — A Complete Windows Guide
entry.content:
@Ahmed_Mosbah 

This is a very impressive and practical example of what MCP can do for SAP development. Connecting Claude directly to an SAP ABAP system makes the workflow feel much more natural, especially when the AI can read, write, and validate code through the system itself instead of relying only on prompts. I like that this goes beyond theory and shows a real integration path that developers can actually learn from. Great work sharing the complete setup and experience.

entry.title: Re: GitHub Copilot for SAP ABAP in VS Code: Setup Guide for On-Premise Teams
entry.content:
@shaikferoz 

This is a very useful setup guide for on-premise ABAP teams. I like that it brings together VS Code, ABAP remote filesystem, and GitHub Copilot in a practical way, because that is exactly the kind of tooling combination many developers are exploring right now. The step-by-step approach makes the adoption path feel much less intimidating, especially for teams moving from traditional ABAP workflows to a more modern coding experience. Great write-up.

entry.title: Re: Agentic Memory Unpacked: Families, Types, Architectures, and Lifecycle
entry.content:
@ladenaw 

This is a really valuable article because it helps make agentic memory much easier to understand. I like that you break it down into families, types, architectures, and lifecycle, since those are exactly the kinds of concepts people need when they are trying to design memory for real AI systems rather than just talk about it in theory. The framing feels practical and well thought out, and it gives readers a much clearer mental model for building memory-enabled agents.

entry.title: Dynamic Criticality in SAP CAP Using Fiori Elements and Node.js
entry.content:
Introduction

While working on a CAP application recently, I came across a requirement where records needed to be highlighted based on their status. The business users wanted a quick way to identify whether an order was completed, still in progress, or blocked without having to read through each record individually.

My first thought was to store a criticality value directly in the database, but that didn't feel like the right approach because the value was purely UI-related and depended on the business status. After exploring a few options, I found that CAP and Fiori Elements provide a clean way to calculate criticality dynamically in the backend and display semantic colors automatically in the UI.

In this blog, I'll walk through the approach I used to achieve this using SAP CAP, Node.js, OData annotations, and Fiori Elements. By the end, you'll have a working example where criticality is calculated at runtime and reflected directly in your Fiori application.

Why Use Dynamic Criticality?

In many business applications, the visual status of a record depends on changing business conditions. For example, an order may move from "In Process" to "Completed", or an expense request may be "Approved" or "Rejected".

Storing criticality values in the database means the values must be maintained whenever the business status changes. By calculating criticality dynamically in the CAP service, the UI always reflects the latest business state while keeping the database model focused on business data.

Step 1: Create a CAP Project

mkdir criticality-demo 
cd criticality-demo 
cds init 
npm install 
npm install sqlite3

Step 2: Create the Data Model

Create the file db/schema.cds

namespace demo.criticality;

entity SalesOrders {
  key ID           : Integer;
      CustomerName : String(100);
      Amount       : Decimal(10,2);
      Currency     : String(3);
      Status       : String(30);
}

Step 3: Add Sample Data

          Create the file:  db/data/demo.criticality-SalesOrders.csv

ID;CustomerName;Amount;Currency;Status
1;ABC Corporation;1200.00;INR;Completed
2;Global Traders;5000.00;INR;In Process
3;Tech Solutions;800.00;INR;Blocked
4;Amazon;900.00;INR;In Process

Step 4: Create an OData Service

          Create the file srv/service.cds.

using demo.criticality as db from '../db/schema';

service CatalogService {
  entity SalesOrders as projection on db.SalesOrders;
}

Step 5: Configure Fiori Annotations

          Create the file app/annotations.cds.

using CatalogService as service from '../srv/service';

annotate service.SalesOrders with @(
  UI.LineItem : [
    {
      $Type : 'UI.DataField',
      Label : 'Customer',
      Value : CustomerName
    },
    {
      $Type : 'UI.DataField',
      Label : 'Amount',
      Value : Amount
    },
    {
      $Type : 'UI.DataField',
      Label : 'Status',
      Value : Status,
      Criticality : Criticality,
      CriticalityRepresentation : #WithIcon
    }
  ],

  UI.SelectionFields : [
    CustomerName,
    Status
  ]
);

          The most important configuration is:

Criticality : Criticality

This tells SAP Fiori to use the Criticality value exposed by the OData service to determine the color and icon displayed for the Status field.

Step 6: Create a Fiori Elements Application

Open the SAP Fiori Application Generator and select:

  • Template: List Report Object Page
  • Data Source: Use a Local CAP Project
  • Service: CatalogService
  • Entity: SalesOrders

Complete the wizard to generate the Fiori Elements application.

Step 7: Run the Application

Start the CAP application:

cds watch

Step 8: Make Criticality Dynamic Using Node.js

Since Criticality is calculated dynamically, we do not need to persist it in the database. Instead, we expose it as a computed field in the service projection and populate it at runtime based on the business status.

A useful side effect of this approach is that if the business decides to change the criticality rules in the future, only the CAP service logic needs to be updated, without requiring database changes or frontend modifications.

At this stage, we will update the service definition created in Step 4 to expose a computed Criticality field.

using demo.criticality as db from '../db/schema';

service CatalogService {
  entity SalesOrders as projection on db.SalesOrders {
    *,
    null as Criticality : Integer
  };
}

           Then Create srv/service.js:

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function () {
  this.after('READ', 'SalesOrders', (orders) => {
    const list = Array.isArray(orders) ? orders : [orders];

    for (const order of list) {
      switch (order.Status) {
        case 'Completed':
          order.Criticality = 3;
          break;
        case 'In Process':
          order.Criticality = 2;
          break;
        case 'Blocked':
          order.Criticality = 1;
          break;
        default:
          order.Criticality = 0;
      }
    }
  });
});

With this implementation, criticality is calculated at runtime based on the business status rather than being stored in the database.

As shown below, Fiori automatically renders different semantic colors based on the Criticality value calculated in the CAP service. Completed orders appear in green, In Process orders appear in yellow, and Blocked orders appear in red.

Understanding Criticality Values

SAP Fiori uses the following standard criticality values:

Criticality Value Meaning Color Typical Usage

0NeutralGrayDefault or informational status
1NegativeRedError, Rejected, Blocked
2CriticalOrange/YellowWarning, Pending, In Process
3PositiveGreenSuccess, Approved, Completed

Conclusion

What I liked about this approach is that the database remains focused on business data while the CAP service handles the UI-specific logic. Instead of maintaining criticality values in a table, we can derive them dynamically from the status and expose them through the service whenever the data is requested.

One thing that took me some time to figure out was that the calculated field must also be exposed in the service projection. Without that step, the application receives the status value but not the criticality information needed for the semantic colors.

Although this example uses sales order statuses, the same pattern can be applied to approval workflows, expense reports, support tickets, or any scenario where users need a quick visual indication of the current state of a record. I hope this helps anyone looking to implement dynamic criticality in their own CAP applications.

entry.title: Re: Teacher's Pension MDC mothballed
entry.content:
As suggested by the note: "Any customer planning to still use the MDC and ASR solutions beyond April 2026 should contact SAP."  The note does not provide an explicit exception or carve-out for customers who are mid-migration or who have a dependency on MDC beyond April 2026 — instead, it directs such customers to contact SAP directly.

So I suggest reaching out to SAP by opening an incident.

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: Temperature-Controlled Shipment Planning in SAP TM — A Complete Configuration Guide SAP Blogs | SAP
entry.content:
Business Scenario

A shipper receives a Sales Order containing multiple products with varying storage conditions:

Product Storage Condition Description

Product AABAmbient
Product BRFRefrigerated (2°C – 8°C)
Product CRTTemperature Controlled (15°C – 25°C)

The system must ensure these products are planned into separate Freight Units and that incompatible storage conditions are never consolidated onto the same transport unit.

Transactional Flow Overview

Here is the end-to-end transactional journey:

 

Step 1: Create a Sales Order with Mixed Storage Conditions

The process begins in SAP S/4HANA (or SAP ECC) where a Sales Order is created with multiple line items, each carrying products that have different storage conditions maintained in the Material Master (MRP2/Plant Data tab).

Key Master Data Prerequisites

  • Material Master → Plant Data / Storage 1 tab: Storage Condition field populated per material.
  • Storage conditions are configured as a custom condition type or use SAP standard condition codes depending on your system setup.

What Happens Here

When the Sales Order is saved and the TM integration is active, the order is forwarded to SAP TM as an Order-Based Transportation Requirement (OBTR) or Delivery-Based Transportation Requirement (DBTR), carrying the storage condition information at the item level.

Step 2: Freight Unit Creation Based on Storage Conditions

Once the transportation requirement arrives in SAP TM, the system applies the Freight Unit Building Rule (FUBR) to create Freight Units (FUs).

The FUBR is configured to split Freight Units by Storage Condition using the Incompatibility Setting. This means:

  • Product A (AB - Ambient) → Freight Unit 1
  • Product B (RF - Refrigerated) → Freight Unit 2
  • Product C (RT - Temperature Controlled) → Freight Unit 3

Each Freight Unit now carries a homogeneous set of products from a storage condition perspective, ready for compatible planning.

Configuration of the FUBR a Incompatibility Setting is assigned to FUBR with the same Logic as planning but the Incompatibility Area= Freight Unit Building and validity of the Incompatibility definition is 

 

 

Step 3: Planning of Freight Units Using Incompatibility Rules

During the planning phase, the SAP TM Optimizer or manual planning tools use the Incompatibility Setting assigned to the Planning Profile to ensure that Freight Units with incompatible storage conditions are never placed on the same Freight Order or Transport Unit.

This acts as a hard block — the system will not allow, even manually, the consolidation of incompatible storage conditions unless the block is explicitly overridden by an authorized user.

Created a Incompatibility Setting with Area as " Automatic Planning and Manual Planning and the Incompatibility definition with below Validity 

 

 

Important to note is the Condition type should be /SCMTMS/INC_FU with identical Values only activated.

entry.title: Auto-Populating Hiring Team Roles from Position Matrix Relationships
entry.content:
The Product Gap

Product Gap: There is currently no standard (out-of-the-box) option to map Position Matrix Relationships from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SmartRecruiters Hiring Team roles. This limitation applies when a job requisition is created from a Position in SmartRecruiters — the matrix relationships (e.g., HR Manager, Dotted-Line Manager) defined on the source Position are not automatically resolved and assigned as Hiring Team members.

This feature is on the SAP product roadmap but is not yet generally available.

In SAP SuccessFactors, the Position Matrix Relationship is a powerful feature that allows organizations to define secondary or indirect reporting relationships alongside the primary line hierarchy. Common examples include HR Business Partners, Dotted-Line Managers, or Functional Leads assigned to a position. These relationships are defined at the Position level and, by extension, apply to the incumbent employee.

In SmartRecruiters, the Hiring Team on a job requisition controls who can view, manage, and collaborate on the requisition. Typical roles include Recruiter, Coordinator, and Hiring Manager. When a requisition is created from a Position in SuccessFactors, it is expected that the right stakeholders — as defined by matrix relationships — would be automatically added to the Hiring Team with the correct roles. This does not happen today via standard integration.

Solution Architecture

 

 

Component Role in the Solution

SmartRecruiters Webhook (job.created)Triggers the automation when a job is created from a Position
EC OData – Job / Position linkRetrieves the Position code tied to the new job
EC OData – PositionMatrixRelationshipReturns all matrix relationships defined on the Position
EC OData – EmpJobFinds the current incumbent on the related/matrix Position
SmartRecruiters Users APIResolves the SuccessFactors user to a SmartRecruiters user ID
SmartRecruiters Hiring Team APIUpserts the resolved user with the correct Hiring Team role
 
Note: The integration layer (webhook listener and orchestration logic) can be implemented on SAP BTP using Integration Suite, Cloud Integration, or a custom Node.js / Python function. The focus of this blog is on the API interactions rather than the middleware implementation.

Step-by-Step Implementation

 

Subscribe to the job.created Webhook in SmartRecruiters

Configure a webhook subscription in SmartRecruiters for the job.created event. Whenever a recruiter creates a new job requisition — particularly one initiated from a Position in SuccessFactors — SmartRecruiters fires this event with the new Job ID in the payload.

 

Figure 1: The job.created webhook payload containing the SmartRecruiters Job ID

From the payload, extract the Job ID (e.g., 2a9e277c-275e-42ad-9749-2137a3a2e9f8). This ID will be used throughout the subsequent steps.

 

Retrieve the Associated Position Code from SmartRecruiters / EC

Using the Job ID returned by the webhook, query the SmartRecruiters or Employee Central API to retrieve the Position code associated with the job. In the standard SuccessFactors–SmartRecruiters integration, the Position code is stored against the job requisition and can be retrieved via the SR Job details endpoint or the EC job requisition linkage.

The Position code is the key that unlocks the matrix relationship query in the next step. Example Position code: 3001511

 

Query Position Matrix Relationships in Employee Central

With the Position code, call the EC OData API endpoint for PositionMatrixRelationship, filtering by the Position code and expanding the related position navigation:

GET https://apisalesdemo8.successfactors.com/odata/v2/PositionMatrixRelationship
    ?$filter=Position_code eq '3001511'
    &$expand=relatedPositionNav

The response contains the matrix relationship type (e.g., hr manager) and the related Position code, which identifies the Position held by the matrix relationship incumbent.

 

 

Figure 2: EC OData API response for PositionMatrixRelationship — showing matrixRelationshipType: hr manager and the related Position

Note the matrixRelationshipType field — this can later be mapped to the corresponding Hiring Team role in SmartRecruiters (e.g., "hr manager" → RECRUITER or a custom role).

 

Identify the Incumbent on the Related Position

Once you have the related Position code from Step 3, query the EmpJob entity in EC to find the current incumbent (employee) holding that position:

GET https://apisalesdemo8.successfactors.com/odata/v2/EmpJob
    ?$filter=position eq '3000775'
    &$select=userId,userNav/displayName
    &$expand=userNav

The response provides the userId of the incumbent in Employee Central (e.g., Tworkshop) along with their display name.

Resolve the SmartRecruiters User ID

SmartRecruiters maintains its own internal user IDs that are different from SuccessFactors user IDs. To upsert a user to the Hiring Team, you need the SR-specific user ID. There are two approaches to resolve this mapping:

Option A — Query the SR Users API: Call the SmartRecruiters Users endpoint and match the employee by email address or name to retrieve their SR user ID.

GET https://api.smartrecruiters.com/users

 

Figure 3: SmartRecruiters Users API response — SR user ID: 6943a4116e5fce00ab01c71c

Option B — Maintain a Custom MDF in SuccessFactors: Run a daily synchronization job that queries the SR Users API and stores the SR user IDs in a custom Metadata Framework (MDF) object in BizX, keyed by the SuccessFactors userId. At runtime, a simple lookup on this MDF returns the SR ID without making an additional API call to SmartRecruiters, reducing latency.

Recommendation: Option B (custom MDF with daily sync) is preferred for production scenarios as it reduces runtime API dependencies and improves reliability. Option A is simpler to implement for proof-of-concept or low-volume scenarios.
 

Upsert the User to the SmartRecruiters Hiring Team

With the SR Job ID (from Step 1) and the SR User ID (from Step 5), make a POST request to the SmartRecruiters Hiring Team endpoint to add the user with the appropriate role:

POST https://api.smartrecruiters.com/jobs/{jobId}/hiring-team

Body:
{
  "id": "6943a4116e5fce00ab01c71c",
  "role": "RECRUITER"
}

The role value should be mapped from the matrixRelationshipType retrieved in Step 3. A suggested mapping is:

 

EC matrixRelationshipType SmartRecruiters Hiring Team Role
hr managerRECRUITER
dotted line managerCOORDINATOR
(custom)(custom mapping as per your configuration)

 

Figure 4: POST to the SmartRecruiters Hiring Team API — 200 OK confirms the user was successfully added

Result

After executing the upsert in Step 6, the SmartRecruiters Hiring Team section on the job requisition is automatically populated with the correct members derived from the Position Matrix Relationships in Employee Central.

In the example below, the hiring team has been updated with two members:

  • Kunal Hasija — Coordinator
  • Test Workshop — Recruiter (added via the API upsert)

 

Figure 5: SmartRecruiters Hiring Team before the upsert — only one member is present

 

Figure 6: SmartRecruiters Hiring Team after the upsert — the matrix relationship incumbent has been added as Recruiter

Outcome: The Hiring Team in SmartRecruiters is now automatically populated based on the Position Matrix Relationships defined in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central — without any manual intervention from the recruiter.

Key Considerations and Recommendations

  • Role Mapping: Define a clear and agreed mapping between EC matrix relationship types and SR Hiring Team roles before implementation. This mapping should be configurable, not hardcoded.
  • User ID Synchronization: Maintaining SR user IDs in a custom MDF in BizX reduces runtime latency and avoids failures caused by SR API rate limits.
  • Multiple Matrix Relationships: A single Position may have multiple matrix relationships. Ensure your implementation iterates over all returned relationships and calls the upsert for each resolved incumbent.
  • Error Handling: If a matrix relationship incumbent is not found in SmartRecruiters (e.g., they are an external contractor not in SR), implement graceful error handling and alerting rather than failing the entire process.
  • Webhook Reliability: Use retry logic and idempotent upsert calls to handle cases where the webhook fires multiple times for the same job creation event.
  • Future Native Support: This gap is on the SAP product roadmap. Monitor SAP release notes and the SAP SuccessFactors Community for updates. Once native support is delivered, the custom solution should be retired to reduce maintenance overhead.

Summary

The absence of a standard mapping between Position Matrix Relationships in SAP SuccessFactors and the SmartRecruiters Hiring Team is a recognized product gap that affects customers using the integrated SuccessFactors–SmartRecruiters talent acquisition setup. Until native support is delivered, the six-step custom solution described in this blog provides a reliable, event-driven workaround:

  1. Subscribe to the SmartRecruiters job.created webhook
  2. Retrieve the Position code associated with the new job
  3. Query EC PositionMatrixRelationship OData API
  4. Identify the incumbent via EC EmpJob OData API
  5. Resolve the SmartRecruiters User ID (via SR Users API or custom MDF)
  6. Upsert the user to the SmartRecruiters Hiring Team with the mapped role

 

Thanks

 

Kunal

Product Success

RIG APJ

entry.title: Re: PORTAL - Understanding the Need for Secure & Responsible AI at SAP
entry.content:
@Ahona_Bhattacharyya-05 

This is an important topic, and I like how the article brings together security, responsibility, and trust in one place. In enterprise AI, those things cannot be treated as optional because the systems often touch sensitive data and real business decisions. The emphasis on human oversight, privacy, and explainability makes the message feel grounded and practical rather than theoretical. Great reminder that responsible AI is not just about innovation — it is about building AI that people can actually trust.

entry.title: Re: Agentic Harness Architecture: Seven Pillars That Make Claude Code Production-Grade
entry.content:
@Mohan-Sharma 

This is a really strong framing of agentic systems. I like the idea that the harness matters more than the raw model, because in production the real challenge is not just intelligence — it is control, context, verification, and workflow design. The seven-pillar approach makes the architecture feel practical and grounded, especially for teams trying to move from experiments to something reliable enough for real work.

entry.title: Re: ABAP FS: Three Months Later
entry.content:
@Aravindh_Ganapathi95 

It’s great to see how much ABAP FS has evolved in just three months. The pace of new features like REPL, notebooks, replay debugger, skills, subagents, and MCP support shows that this is becoming much more than a simple developer tool — it’s turning into a real AI-assisted development environment for ABAP. What stands out to me is that it seems focused on making ABAP work feel more modern while still staying practical for real SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Building an Autonomous SAP Joule Agent for ServiceNow ITSM on SAP BTP
entry.content:
@AmitBangotra 

This is a great example of what Joule Studio can do when it is applied to a real enterprise use case. I like that the article goes beyond a simple chatbot idea and shows how an autonomous Joule Agent can actually help with ServiceNow ITSM on SAP BTP. That makes the concept feel much more practical and relevant for teams trying to automate real workflows, not just demos.

entry.title: Re: Machine Telepathy - How Latent Memory Links Agents Without Words
entry.content:
@Akshat6 

This is a fascinating idea. I like how the article explores latent memory as a way for agents to stay connected without constantly exchanging words, because that gets closer to how coordination might work in more advanced multi-agent systems. The concept feels both creative and technically meaningful, especially for people thinking about agent collaboration, memory, and long-lived context. It is a nice reminder that AI communication does not always have to be text-based to be effective.

entry.title: Re: AI Product Management: The Four Things Every Agent Needs Before It Ships
entry.content:
@yoramfriedman 

This is a strong framing of what it takes to ship agents responsibly. I like that it focuses on the practical foundations — clear success criteria, memory decisions, operational planning, and better metrics — because those are the things that usually determine whether an agent works in the real world. The reminder to start small, keep humans in the loop, and test outside the lab makes the advice feel grounded rather than hype-driven.

entry.title: Re: Product-Agent Fit: A Practical Framework for Building on SAP BTP in the Age of Joule
entry.content:
@ZYT 

This is a really useful framework because it shifts the discussion from “can we build an agent?” to “should this product be agentic at all, and what would make it fit for that?” I like that it grounds the idea in SAP BTP, since that makes the guidance feel practical for enterprise teams that need to balance innovation with architecture, governance, and real business value. It reads less like a trend piece and more like a decision framework for building responsibly.

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule Document Grounding with Google Drive - Connecting AI to Enterprise Knowledge (Video De
entry.content:
@MrRaj 

This is a very practical example of Joule becoming more useful in the enterprise. I like that the article focuses on grounding answers in Google Drive content, because that makes the assistant feel less generic and much more trustworthy for real business knowledge. It also shows a clear path from “AI can chat” to “AI can answer from our own documents,” which is where the real value starts to show up.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM DODO — The 4 Variants You Must Know Before You Configure (DODO in Embedded S/4HANA 2025
entry.content:
Hi Aleš, just to close the loop — I retested with the correct /SPE/DIRDLV_TYPE entry and can confirm Variant 3 (ODSH → STO) works fully in S/4HANA 2025 as well. STO and outbound delivery created via the same qRFC path as OPIG, GI confirmed end-to-end. The earlier finding was a configuration gap in my sandbox, not a platform limitation as you correctly suspected. I will update the blog with the verified config path. Thank you for the push — this makes the framework more complete and accurate for everyone.

BR Yogesh

entry.title: Re: EPPM Business Requirements , S4 Hana Private cloud
entry.content: Can you elaborate what CRM functionality they mentioned?

entry.title: Re: AI Product Management: The Cost Model Your Business Case Is Missing
entry.content:
@yoramfriedman 

This is a really important angle because AI business cases often underestimate the true cost of running agents. I like that the article calls out the hidden operational and runtime costs, since those are usually what separate a nice demo from something that can actually scale. It makes the argument feel more realistic and useful for anyone trying to justify AI investment with honest numbers.

entry.title: Re: From Manual Testing to AI-Assisted QA in SAP: A Practical Journey
entry.content:
@Divita 

This is a thoughtful and honest take on AI-assisted QA, especially because it does not pretend AI solves everything. I like that it highlights both the gains and the limits, since that is exactly what teams need when they are moving from manual testing to a more intelligent QA workflow. The practical tone makes it feel credible and useful for SAP teams trying to improve testing without losing control.

entry.title: Re: Restart Failed E-Signatures Without Restarting the Entire Onboarding Process post 1H 2026 Releas
entry.content:
@Radhika_Windlass03 
Onboarding document flow step restart works in below cases:

  1.     If document flow status is completed or skipped, user can restart the document flow.
  2.     If document flow is triggered after release and is in status in-progress, user can restart the document flow.
  3.     If document flow was triggered before release and is still in-progress after release, user will not be able to restart this document flow. This document flow needs to be declined to regenerate the documents again.

Pre-requisites are mentioned here: Restart e-Signature Step for Document Flows

entry.title: Re: Production Agentic SAP Systems: Monitoring, Cost, Failure, and Scale (Series 2, Part 6 - complet
entry.content:
@DEEPA_DORAIRAJ 

This is one of the most practical angles in the whole agent conversation because it focuses on what happens after launch. I like that it calls out monitoring, cost control, failure handling, and scale, since those are the issues that usually decide whether an agent stays useful in production or becomes a maintenance burden. The article feels especially relevant for SAP teams because it treats agents as operational systems, not just demos.

entry.title: Re: How SAP Joule is Transforming the Developer Experience
entry.content:
@Smriti_Chandra 

This is a strong overview of how Joule is changing the developer experience on SAP. I like that it focuses on practical help like code suggestions, unit test generation, and migration support, because those are the kinds of things developers actually feel day to day. The article makes the shift feel concrete: AI is not just a chat layer anymore, but part of the development workflow itself.

entry.title: Re: How to Set Up a Python Virtual Environment
entry.content:
@Trupti81 

This is a great practical refresher because it keeps the focus on one of the simplest habits that saves developers a lot of trouble. I like that it explains virtual environments in plain language, since isolating dependencies is one of those basics that can prevent a lot of setup headaches later. The article feels especially useful for people who want a clean, repeatable Python workflow.

entry.title: Re: Orchestrating Autonomous Coding Agents - Manage Your Personal AI Dev Team!
entry.content:
@ladenaw 

This is a really interesting look at how autonomous coding agents can be coordinated like a small dev team. I like that it covers both the upside and the pitfalls, because orchestration is where the real complexity starts once you move beyond a single agent. The article feels useful for anyone experimenting with agent workflows and trying to keep speed, quality, and control in balance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule- The "Totally not an official ITSM connector"
entry.content:
@AmitBangotra 

This is a clever and memorable way to explain how Joule can sit in the middle of SAP and ServiceNow workflows without pretending to be a native ITSM product. I like the framing because it makes the integration idea feel practical: Joule can translate requests into APIs, structure incidents, and keep workflows moving without adding unnecessary complexity. The playful title works well, but the underlying message is serious and useful for anyone building cross-system automation.

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL Server Setup
entry.content:
@Trupti81 

This is a very practical setup guide, and I like that it focuses on the basics instead of assuming everything is already configured. Clear steps for installation, connection, and local development make it useful for anyone trying to get PostgreSQL running on SAP BTP without a lot of trial and error. It feels like the kind of article that saves time the first time you set things up.

entry.title: Re: Human-in-the-Loop SAP Agents: Approval, Escalation, and Audit (Series 2, Part 5 of 6)
entry.content:
@DEEPA_DORAIRAJ 

This is a really important part of building agents in the real world, because autonomy only works when there is a clear approval path, escalation logic, and audit trail behind it. I like that the article treats human-in-the-loop as a design pattern rather than a checkbox, since that is what makes agent behavior controllable and trustworthy in production. It feels especially relevant for SAP systems, where the stakes for business actions are high.

entry.title: Re: Working with Git commands
entry.content:
@Trupti81 

This is a helpful beginner-friendly guide because it turns Git into something less intimidating and more practical. I like that it starts with the core commands — init, add, commit, and push — since those are the building blocks people actually need to get comfortable with first. The article feels like a good starting point for anyone working in SAP Business Application Studio or learning Git for the first time.

entry.title: Re: Building AI Agents and Agentic Workflows with Human-in-the-Loop: A Beginner's Guide to Mastra
entry.content:
@thatisuday 

This is a great beginner-friendly introduction because it makes agentic workflows feel approachable instead of overwhelming. I like that it emphasizes human-in-the-loop checkpoints, since that is one of the most important ideas for building agents that are actually safe and useful in practice. The practical, step-by-step framing makes it a solid starting point for anyone learning how to combine autonomy with oversight.

entry.title: Re: How I can control MOQ in a sales order not by SKU but all order

entry.title: Re: EVENT_PARAMETER cuts long strings at 255 characters
entry.content: I mean the datatype uniqueidentifier and the newid() method to generate.

entry.title: Re: New SDK Capabilities: Filter by Range and Filter by Condition for SAC Custom Widgets
entry.content:
@Michelle_Miley 

This is a useful update because it makes SAC custom widgets more flexible without forcing everything into scripting workarounds. I like that the new filter-by-range and filter-by-condition capabilities make filtering feel more natural and practical for real dashboards. It seems especially helpful for teams building custom analytics experiences where better filtering can make the whole story easier to use.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content:
thank you for your input @JanStauffer @HarshPatel @ankurch @Kerisha123 @Chaimae_Chairi27 and @CharlesGirdwood 
I am tallying the results and will report back soon!

entry.title: Re: Create a Custom SAP BDC Data Product share it with Enterprise Databricks, extend it and send it
entry.content:
@gvglupita 

This is a nice example of how SAP Business Data Cloud can fit into a broader data ecosystem instead of sitting in isolation. I like that the article shows a practical path for creating a custom data product and sharing it with Enterprise Databricks, because that makes the integration story feel concrete and useful. It reads like the kind of guide that helps teams move from concept to something they can actually use.

entry.title: Re: How to create an Aging Analysis report using a User selected Key Date leveraging SAC Date differ
entry.content:
@N1kh1l 

This is a practical workaround for a common reporting need, and I like that it focuses on making the aging analysis respond to a user-selected key date. That makes the report much more useful for real business review scenarios, where “as of” dates matter. The article feels especially helpful for SAC users who need a flexible solution without overcomplicating the model.

entry.title: Re: From Numbers to Narrative: Building an AI Analytics Assistant in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@spurwar 

Really insightful work. I like how you turned SAC into a practical AI assistant that blends analysis, narratives, and visuals so smoothly. The PowerPoint export and persona‑based summaries are especially impressive. Thanks for sharing this approach.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
entry.content: 68fc8275a2d6acf5de63072d729d2a96baa0b8c2e8d8a453a8a6b26e8e6159bd

entry.title: Re: Adding a Decomposition Tree to SAP Analytics Cloud with D3.js
entry.content:
@abhimanyu_sharma 

Really cool implementation. Bringing a decomposition tree into SAC using D3.js is a smart way to fill a key visualization gap, and the drill‑down experience looks smooth and intuitive. Love seeing creative extensions like this that push SAC’s capabilities forward.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content: Fortunately, we are still using basic authentication and not IAS so I still rely heavily on email services for access the candidate email.  We will have to switch to IAS later this year but I think it is going to cause alot of problems with rehires and generally a not great new hire experience.  I'm investigating how best to mitigate the damage from switching over to IAS.

entry.title: Re: Building a Production-Ready SAC Custom Widget with Claude AI — From Zero to Four Widgets
entry.content:
@Michelle_Miley 

Really well done. This is a great walkthrough of how to take a custom SAC widget from prototype to production using Claude. The way you structured the development flow, testing, and deployment makes the whole process feel much more approachable. Thanks for sharing such a practical, real‑world example.
